message:
refactor(recorder_migration): use tick-74 RecorderStorage _with_cx siblings (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Follow-on to tick 74. Wires the new `RecorderStorage` trait
_with_cx default methods into the tick 67-71 migration
methods. Each replacement swaps `cx.checkpoint()?; storage.X()`
for `storage.X_with_cx(cx)` — same cancellation seam, cleaner
call site, AND gives backends with internal cx support a place
to hook in (default impl is observationally equivalent to the
previous pattern).

Changes (all semantic-preserving — all 56 legacy + cx-first
migration tests still pass):

  * `MigrationEngine::m2_import_with_cx` —
    `target.append_batch(req)` →
    `target.append_batch_with_cx(cx, req)`. The outer per-chunk
    `cx.checkpoint()?` stays because it embeds chunk-counter
    context in the error message.

  * `MigrationEngine::m3_checkpoint_sync_with_cx` — three
    swaps:
      - pre-flight `cx.checkpoint()?; source.lag_metrics()` →
        `source.lag_metrics_with_cx(cx)` (absorbs the explicit
        pre-flight checkpoint into the trait default's seam).
      - per-consumer `cx.checkpoint()?; source.read_checkpoint()`
        → `source.read_checkpoint_with_cx(cx, ...)`. Drops the
        per-iteration running-counter context from the
        cancellation error; operators can inspect `result` state
        for progress instead.
      - `target.commit_checkpoint()` →
        `target.commit_checkpoint_with_cx(cx, ...)`.

  * `MigrationEngine::m5_cutover_with_cx` —
    `target.append_batch(req)` →
    `target.append_batch_with_cx(cx, req)`. The post-append
    `cx.checkpoint()?` stays (it gates the health check —
    separate concern from the append).

`m0_preflight_with_cx` intentionally NOT refactored — its
`source.health()` call has no `_with_cx` sibling by design (see
tick 74 rationale: health returns cached state, no backing
I/O).

Rationale for keeping SOME explicit `cx.checkpoint()?` calls:
where the checkpoint error message carries important
iteration-context (chunk counter, final_ordinal), the explicit
call provides better operator UX than the generic
"append_batch cancelled" that comes from the trait default.
Where the context is redundant (pre-flight with no running
counters yet), the explicit checkpoint is absorbed into the
trait sibling for cleaner code.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * recorder_migration:: — 56 passed / 0 failed (unchanged
    count from tick 74; 10 Cx-first tests + 46 legacy tests
    all still green — proves the refactor preserved semantics
    across BOTH legacy and Cx-first test paths)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Line delta: 34 additions, 16 deletions (+18 net). The
additions are new doc comments explaining the refactor's
intent; actual code got shorter.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
